# Flotsam — Claude Instructions

Personal knowledge management system: a monorepo with a Go API (`api/`) and a
React/TypeScript frontend (`web/`). Component-specific deep-dives are in the
`@import` files below.

---

## Prerequisites

- **Go** 1.24+
- **Node.js** 22+ with **pnpm** 9+ (`npm install -g pnpm`)
- **Docker** (for `make dev` — runs PostgreSQL + pgvector + MinIO in containers)
- **PostgreSQL** 16+ with pgvector (only if managing DB manually instead of `make dev`)
- **golangci-lint** (optional): see golangci-lint docs

---

## Environment setup

```bash
cp .env.example .env   # fill in DATABASE_URL and JWT_SECRET at minimum
```

Required: `DATABASE_URL` — the API refuses to start without it.

Ports:
- **API**: `:8081`
- **Web dev server**: `:8080` — proxies `/graphql`, `/api`, and `/mcp` → `localhost:8081`

---

## Commands (Root Makefile)

| Command | Action |
|---|---|
| `make dev` | Full-stack dev environment (Docker PG + air + Vite) — single command |
| `make dev-db-up` | Start dev database only (PG 16 + pgvector on port 5433) |
| `make dev-db-down` | Stop dev database |
| `make api-build` | Build all API binaries (flotsamd, flotsam-worker, flotsam) |
| `make api-run` | Run API server |
| `make api-generate` | Regenerate gqlgen code |
| `make api-test` | All Go tests (race detector) |
| `make api-test-unit` | Unit tests only (`-short`) |
| `make lint` | Run all linters (Go + web) |
| `make api-lint` | go vet + golangci-lint (includes gosec) |
| `make api-migrate-up` | Apply pending migrations |
| `make api-migrate-down` | Roll back last migration |
| `make web-install` | `pnpm install` |
| `make web-dev` | Vite dev server (port 8080) |
| `make web-build` | TypeScript check + production build |
| `make web-lint` | ESLint (zero-warning mode) |
| `make web-test` | Vitest unit tests |
| `make api-test-tdd` | API tests with tdd-guard-go (when installed) |
| `make web-test-tdd` | Web tests with TDD Guard reporter (when configured) |
| `make web-schema-sync` | Regenerate `web/schema.graphql` + `graphql-env.d.ts` from API SDL |
| `make test-quick` | Fast checks: Go unit tests + TS build + ESLint (~10-20s, no DB) |
| `make test-standard` | Full local: quick + web unit tests + Go lint (~30-60s, no DB) |
| `make test-full` | Complete: standard + integration + e2e (requires DB + services) |

# Flotsam — Claude Instructions

Personal knowledge management system: a monorepo with a Go API (`api/`) and a
React/TypeScript frontend (`web/`). Component-specific deep-dives are in the
`@import` files below.

---

## Prerequisites

- **Go** 1.24+
- **Node.js** 22+ with **pnpm** 9+ (`npm install -g pnpm`)
- **Docker** (for `make dev` — runs PostgreSQL + pgvector + MinIO in containers)
- **PostgreSQL** 16+ with pgvector (only if managing DB manually instead of `make dev`)
- **golangci-lint** (optional): see golangci-lint docs

---

## Environment setup

```bash
cp .env.example .env   # fill in DATABASE_URL and JWT_SECRET at minimum
```

Required: `DATABASE_URL` — the API refuses to start without it.

Ports:
- **API**: `:8081`
- **Web dev server**: `:8080` — proxies `/graphql`, `/api`, and `/mcp` → `localhost:8081`

---

## Commands (Root Makefile)

| Command | Action |
|---|---|
| `make dev` | Full-stack dev environment (Docker PG + air + Vite) — single command |
| `make dev-db-up` | Start dev database only (PG 16 + pgvector on port 5433) |
| `make dev-db-down` | Stop dev database |
| `make api-build` | Build all API binaries (flotsamd, flotsam-worker, flotsam) |
| `make api-run` | Run API server |
| `make api-generate` | Regenerate gqlgen code |
| `make api-test` | All Go tests (race detector) |
| `make api-test-unit` | Unit tests only (`-short`) |
| `make lint` | Run all linters (Go + web) |
| `make api-lint` | go vet + golangci-lint (includes gosec) |
| `make api-migrate-up` | Apply pending migrations |
| `make api-migrate-down` | Roll back last migration |
| `make web-install` | `pnpm install` |
| `make web-dev` | Vite dev server (port 8080) |
| `make web-build` | TypeScript check + production build |
| `make web-lint` | ESLint (zero-warning mode) |
| `make web-test` | Vitest unit tests |
| `make api-test-tdd` | API tests with tdd-guard-go (when installed) |
| `make web-test-tdd` | Web tests with TDD Guard reporter (when configured) |
| `make web-schema-sync` | Regenerate `web/schema.graphql` + `graphql-env.d.ts` from API SDL |
| `make test-quick` | Fast checks: Go unit tests + TS build + ESLint (~10-20s, no DB) |
| `make test-standard` | Full local: quick + web unit tests + Go lint (~30-60s, no DB) |
| `make test-full` | Complete: standard + integration + e2e (requires DB + services) |

---

## Testing standards

### Test tiers

| Tier | Command | Time | External deps | Use when |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Quick | `make test-quick` | ~10-20s | None | During development |
| Standard | `make test-standard` | ~30-60s | None | Before committing |
| Full | `make test-full` | ~2-5min | DB + running services | Before merge |

### Core rules

- **Always write tests for new additions.** Tests ship with the code, not as a follow-up.
- **Update existing tests when modifying behaviour.**
- **Target >= 80% line coverage per package.** Glue/wiring packages (`cmd/server`, `router`) are exempt.
- **Always run database code against a real database.** Compilation and unit tests alone are not sufficient — runtime issues like pgx type encoding mismatches only surface with a live connection.

---

## Issue tracking (Beads)

```bash
bd list                                # all open issues
bd ready                               # issues with no blockers (ready to work)
bd show <id>                           # detailed view with dependencies
bd create --title "..." --description "..."
bd update <id> --status in_progress
bd close <id>
```

See `AGENTS.md` for issue type guidance, epic creation, dependencies, and
completion workflow.

---

## Common task recipes

### Add a config env var

1. Add field to `Config` struct in `api/internal/config/config.go` with `env:"VAR_NAME"` tag
2. Document it in `.env.example`
3. Access via `cfg.FieldName` (config flows through `router.Deps`)

### Add a database query

Use pgx directly: `pool.QueryRow(ctx, sql, $1, $2, ...)`. Always pass context.
Never build SQL with `fmt.Sprintf` — always use `$N` placeholders.

### Add a Go package

Create `api/internal/<name>/` with `package <name>`. Pass deps explicitly via
structs; no `init()` globals.

---

## Git workflow

When work is complete and verified, merge directly into `main` from the command
line. Do not create pull requests unless there is an unresolvable merge problem.

See `AGENTS.md` for commit conventions, worktree workflow, and session
completion protocol.

---

## Implementation plans

After plan mode approval, copy the plan from `~/.claude/plans/` into
`docs/plans/` with a meaningful name (e.g., `flt-a1b-db-schema.md`).
Plans in `~/.claude/` are machine-local; the repo copy syncs across devices.
See `AGENTS.md` for naming conventions and lifecycle.

---

## Project specs (`docs/specs/`)

Comprehensive product and architecture documentation lives in `docs/specs/`.
Consult these before making significant changes to understand current behaviour.

| File | Covers |
|---|---|
| `product-overview.md` | Architecture, data model, API design, auth, MCP, tech choices |

---

- **Never edit** `graph/generated/generated.go` or `graph/model/models_gen.go` — run `make api-generate`
- **Never commit** `.env` — only `.env.example`
- **Never use `fmt.Sprintf` to build SQL** — use parameterized `$N` placeholders
- **Never hardcode brand colors** — use Mantine color refs or CSS vars
- **Never use Tailwind utility classes** — use Mantine style props or inline styles
- **Never add** `node_modules/` or `dist/` to git

`README.md` is the human-facing project documentation. **Keep it up to date**
when making changes that affect how someone builds, runs, or configures the
project.

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Check test coverage for Go and web components.
# Usage: check-coverage.sh
#
# Environment:
#   FLOTSAM_STRICT=1  — exit non-zero on any failure (default: advisory)
set -euo pipefail

repo_root="$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)"
cd "$repo_root"

STRICT="${FLOTSAM_STRICT:-0}"
GO_THRESHOLD=80
FAILURES=0

# --- Go coverage ---
echo "=== Go Coverage (threshold: ${GO_THRESHOLD}%) ==="

# Exempt packages: wiring-only, generated code, test utilities, CLI commands
EXEMPT_PATTERNS=(
    "cmd/"
    "internal/router"
    "internal/server"
    "internal/testutil"
    "graph/generated"
    "graph/model"
)

cd api
go test -short -coverprofile=coverage.out ./... > /dev/null 2>&1 || true

if [[ ! -f coverage.out ]]; then
    echo "WARNING: No coverage profile generated"
    FAILURES=1
else
    go_failed=0
    go_passed=0
    go_exempt=0

    while IFS= read -r line; do
        # Lines look like: github.com/ketang/flotsam/api/internal/auth/auth.go:42:  Validate  100.0%
        # The summary line looks like: total:  (statements)  85.2%
        # We want per-package totals from `go tool cover -func`
        # but it only gives per-function. Use a different approach.
        true
    done < <(go tool cover -func=coverage.out)

    # Parse per-package coverage from the profile using go tool cover
    # Extract unique packages and their coverage from the total line per package
    declare -A pkg_coverage
    while IFS= read -r line; do
        # Extract package path and coverage from func output
        if [[ "$line" == total:* ]]; then
            continue
        fi
        # Lines: github.com/ketang/flotsam/api/internal/auth/auth.go:10: FuncName 85.0%
        file_path=$(echo "$line" | awk '{print $1}' | cut -d: -f1)
        if [[ -n "$file_path" ]]; then
            # Extract package from file path (remove filename)
            pkg=$(dirname "$file_path" | sed "s|github.com/ketang/flotsam/api/||")
            pkg_coverage["$pkg"]=1
        fi
    done < <(go tool cover -func=coverage.out)

    # Get per-package coverage percentages using go test -cover
    while IFS= read -r line; do
        # Lines like: ok  github.com/ketang/flotsam/api/internal/auth  0.5s  coverage: 85.2% of statements
        # or: ?   github.com/ketang/flotsam/api/internal/foo  [no test files]
        if [[ "$line" == *"coverage:"* ]]; then
            pkg=$(echo "$line" | awk '{print $2}' | sed "s|github.com/ketang/flotsam/api/||")
            pct=$(echo "$line" | grep -oP 'coverage: \K[0-9.]+')

            # Check exemptions
            exempt=false
            for pattern in "${EXEMPT_PATTERNS[@]}"; do
                if [[ "$pkg" == $pattern* ]]; then
                    exempt=true
                    break
                fi
            done

            if $exempt; then
                printf "  %-40s %6s%%  (exempt)\n" "$pkg" "$pct"
                ((go_exempt++))
            elif (( $(echo "$pct < $GO_THRESHOLD" | bc -l) )); then
                printf "  %-40s %6s%%  FAIL (< %d%%)\n" "$pkg" "$pct" "$GO_THRESHOLD"
                ((go_failed++))
            else
                printf "  %-40s %6s%%  ok\n" "$pkg" "$pct"
                ((go_passed++))
            fi
        elif [[ "$line" == *"[no test files]"* ]]; then
            pkg=$(echo "$line" | awk '{print $2}' | sed "s|github.com/ketang/flotsam/api/||")

            exempt=false
            for pattern in "${EXEMPT_PATTERNS[@]}"; do
                if [[ "$pkg" == $pattern* ]]; then
                    exempt=true
                    break
                fi
            done

            if $exempt; then
                printf "  %-40s %6s   (exempt, no tests)\n" "$pkg" "-"
                ((go_exempt++))
            else
                printf "  %-40s %6s   WARN (no test files)\n" "$pkg" "-"
            fi
        fi
    done < <(go test -short -cover ./... 2>&1)

    rm -f coverage.out

    echo ""
    echo "Go summary: ${go_passed} passed, ${go_failed} failed, ${go_exempt} exempt"
    if [[ "$go_failed" -gt 0 ]]; then
        FAILURES=1
    fi
fi

cd "$repo_root"
echo ""

# --- Web coverage ---
echo "=== Web Coverage (threshold: 80% — enforced by Vitest) ==="

cd web
if pnpm test:coverage 2>&1; then
    echo "Web coverage: ok"
else
    echo "Web coverage: FAIL"
    FAILURES=1
fi

cd "$repo_root"
echo ""

# --- Summary ---
if [[ "$FAILURES" -gt 0 ]]; then
    if [[ "$STRICT" == "1" ]]; then
        echo "COVERAGE FAILED — strict mode, exiting with error."
        exit 1
    else
        echo "Coverage issues found (advisory mode — not blocking)."
        exit 0
    fi
else
    echo "All coverage checks passed."
    exit 0
fi

content.text: The issue is that `((go_exempt++))` returns exit code 1 when the variable is 0 (bash arithmetic quirk with `set -e`). Also the script is overly complex — it runs `go test` twice and has dead code. Let me rewrite it cleanly.

input.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Check test coverage for Go and web components.
# Usage: check-coverage.sh
#
# Environment:
#   FLOTSAM_STRICT=1  — exit non-zero on any failure (default: advisory)
set -euo pipefail

repo_root="$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)"
cd "$repo_root"

STRICT="${FLOTSAM_STRICT:-0}"
GO_THRESHOLD=80
FAILURES=0

# --- Go coverage ---
echo "=== Go Coverage (threshold: ${GO_THRESHOLD}%) ==="

# Exempt packages: wiring-only, generated code, test utilities, CLI commands
EXEMPT_PATTERNS=(
    "cmd/"
    "internal/router"
    "internal/server"
    "internal/testutil"
    "graph/generated"
    "graph/model"
)

is_exempt() {
    local pkg="$1"
    for pattern in "${EXEMPT_PATTERNS[@]}"; do
        if [[ "$pkg" == $pattern* ]]; then
            return 0
        fi
    done
    return 1
}

go_failed=0
go_passed=0
go_exempt=0

cd api
while IFS= read -r line; do
    if [[ "$line" == *"coverage:"* ]]; then
        pkg=$(echo "$line" | awk '{print $2}' | sed "s|github.com/ketang/flotsam/api/||")
        pct=$(echo "$line" | grep -oP 'coverage: \K[0-9.]+')

        if is_exempt "$pkg"; then
            printf "  %-40s %6s%%  (exempt)\n" "$pkg" "$pct"
            go_exempt=$((go_exempt + 1))
        elif (( $(echo "$pct < $GO_THRESHOLD" | bc -l) )); then
            printf "  %-40s %6s%%  FAIL (< %d%%)\n" "$pkg" "$pct" "$GO_THRESHOLD"
            go_failed=$((go_failed + 1))
        else
            printf "  %-40s %6s%%  ok\n" "$pkg" "$pct"
            go_passed=$((go_passed + 1))
        fi
    elif [[ "$line" == *"[no test files]"* ]]; then
        pkg=$(echo "$line" | awk '{print $2}' | sed "s|github.com/ketang/flotsam/api/||")

        if is_exempt "$pkg"; then
            printf "  %-40s %6s   (exempt, no tests)\n" "$pkg" "-"
            go_exempt=$((go_exempt + 1))
        else
            printf "  %-40s %6s   WARN (no test files)\n" "$pkg" "-"
        fi
    fi
done < <(go test -short -cover ./... 2>&1 || true)

cd "$repo_root"

echo ""
echo "Go summary: ${go_passed} passed, ${go_failed} failed, ${go_exempt} exempt"
if [[ "$go_failed" -gt 0 ]]; then
    FAILURES=1
fi
echo ""

# --- Web coverage ---

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Check test coverage for Go and web components.
# Usage: check-coverage.sh
#
# Environment:
#   FLOTSAM_STRICT=1  — exit non-zero on any failure (default: advisory)
set -euo pipefail

repo_root="$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)"
cd "$repo_root"

STRICT="${FLOTSAM_STRICT:-0}"
GO_THRESHOLD=80
FAILURES=0

# --- Go coverage ---
echo "=== Go Coverage (threshold: ${GO_THRESHOLD}%) ==="

# Exempt packages: wiring-only, generated code, test utilities, CLI commands
EXEMPT_PATTERNS=(
    "cmd/"
    "internal/router"
    "internal/server"
    "internal/testutil"
    "graph/generated"
    "graph/model"
)

cd api
go test -short -coverprofile=coverage.out ./... > /dev/null 2>&1 || true

if [[ ! -f coverage.out ]]; then
    echo "WARNING: No coverage profile generated"
    FAILURES=1
else
    go_failed=0
    go_passed=0
    go_exempt=0

    while IFS= read -r line; do
        # Lines look like: github.com/ketang/flotsam/api/internal/auth/auth.go:42:  Validate  100.0%
        # The summary line looks like: total:  (statements)  85.2%
        # We want per-package totals from `go tool cover -func`
        # but it only gives per-function. Use a different approach.
        true
    done < <(go tool cover -func=coverage.out)

    # Parse per-package coverage from the profile using go tool cover
    # Extract unique packages and their coverage from the total line per package
    declare -A pkg_coverage
    while IFS= read -r line; do
        # Extract package path and coverage from func output
        if [[ "$line" == total:* ]]; then
            continue
        fi
        # Lines: github.com/ketang/flotsam/api/internal/auth/auth.go:10: FuncName 85.0%
        file_path=$(echo "$line" | awk '{print $1}' | cut -d: -f1)
        if [[ -n "$file_path" ]]; then
            # Extract package from file path (remove filename)
            pkg=$(dirname "$file_path" | sed "s|github.com/ketang/flotsam/api/||")
            pkg_coverage["$pkg"]=1
        fi
    done < <(go tool cover -func=coverage.out)

    # Get per-package coverage percentages using go test -cover
    while IFS= read -r line; do
        # Lines like: ok  github.com/ketang/flotsam/api/internal/auth  0.5s  coverage: 85.2% of statements
        # or: ?   github.com/ketang/flotsam/api/internal/foo  [no test files]
        if [[ "$line" == *"coverage:"* ]]; then
            pkg=$(echo "$line" | awk '{print $2}' | sed "s|github.com/ketang/flotsam/api/||")
            pct=$(echo "$line" | grep -oP 'coverage: \K[0-9.]+')

            # Check exemptions
            exempt=false
            for pattern in "${EXEMPT_PATTERNS[@]}"; do
                if [[ "$pkg" == $pattern* ]]; then
                    exempt=true
                    break
                fi
            done

            if $exempt; then
                printf "  %-40s %6s%%  (exempt)\n" "$pkg" "$pct"
                ((go_exempt++))
            elif (( $(echo "$pct < $GO_THRESHOLD" | bc -l) )); then
                printf "  %-40s %6s%%  FAIL (< %d%%)\n" "$pkg" "$pct" "$GO_THRESHOLD"
                ((go_failed++))
            else
                printf "  %-40s %6s%%  ok\n" "$pkg" "$pct"
                ((go_passed++))
            fi
        elif [[ "$line" == *"[no test files]"* ]]; then
            pkg=$(echo "$line" | awk '{print $2}' | sed "s|github.com/ketang/flotsam/api/||")

            exempt=false
            for pattern in "${EXEMPT_PATTERNS[@]}"; do
                if [[ "$pkg" == $pattern* ]]; then
                    exempt=true
                    break
                fi
            done

            if $exempt; then
                printf "  %-40s %6s   (exempt, no tests)\n" "$pkg" "-"
                ((go_exempt++))
            else
                printf "  %-40s %6s   WARN (no test files)\n" "$pkg" "-"
            fi
        fi
    done < <(go test -short -cover ./... 2>&1)

    rm -f coverage.out

    echo ""
    echo "Go summary: ${go_passed} passed, ${go_failed} failed, ${go_exempt} exempt"
    if [[ "$go_failed" -gt 0 ]]; then
        FAILURES=1
    fi
fi

cd "$repo_root"
echo ""

# --- Web coverage ---
echo "=== Web Coverage (threshold: 80% — enforced by Vitest) ==="

cd web
if pnpm test:coverage 2>&1; then
    echo "Web coverage: ok"
else
    echo "Web coverage: FAIL"
    FAILURES=1
fi

cd "$repo_root"
echo ""

# --- Summary ---
if [[ "$FAILURES" -gt 0 ]]; then
    if [[ "$STRICT" == "1" ]]; then
        echo "COVERAGE FAILED — strict mode, exiting with error."
        exit 1
    else
        echo "Coverage issues found (advisory mode — not blocking)."
        exit 0
    fi
else
    echo "All coverage checks passed."
    exit 0
fi

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Check test coverage for Go and web components.
# Usage: check-coverage.sh
#
# Environment:
#   FLOTSAM_STRICT=1  — exit non-zero on any failure (default: advisory)
set -euo pipefail

repo_root="$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)"
cd "$repo_root"

STRICT="${FLOTSAM_STRICT:-0}"
GO_THRESHOLD=80
FAILURES=0

# --- Go coverage ---
echo "=== Go Coverage (threshold: ${GO_THRESHOLD}%) ==="

# Exempt packages: wiring-only, generated code, test utilities, CLI commands
EXEMPT_PATTERNS=(
    "cmd/"
    "internal/router"
    "internal/server"
    "internal/testutil"
    "graph/generated"
    "graph/model"
)

is_exempt() {
    local pkg="$1"
    for pattern in "${EXEMPT_PATTERNS[@]}"; do
        if [[ "$pkg" == $pattern* ]]; then
            return 0
        fi
    done
    return 1
}

go_failed=0
go_passed=0
go_exempt=0

cd api
while IFS= read -r line; do
    if [[ "$line" == *"coverage:"* ]]; then
        pkg=$(echo "$line" | awk '{print $2}' | sed "s|github.com/ketang/flotsam/api/||")
        pct=$(echo "$line" | grep -oP 'coverage: \K[0-9.]+')

        if is_exempt "$pkg"; then
            printf "  %-40s %6s%%  (exempt)\n" "$pkg" "$pct"
            go_exempt=$((go_exempt + 1))
        elif (( $(echo "$pct < $GO_THRESHOLD" | bc -l) )); then
            printf "  %-40s %6s%%  FAIL (< %d%%)\n" "$pkg" "$pct" "$GO_THRESHOLD"
            go_failed=$((go_failed + 1))
        else
            printf "  %-40s %6s%%  ok\n" "$pkg" "$pct"
            go_passed=$((go_passed + 1))
        fi
    elif [[ "$line" == *"[no test files]"* ]]; then
        pkg=$(echo "$line" | awk '{print $2}' | sed "s|github.com/ketang/flotsam/api/||")

        if is_exempt "$pkg"; then
            printf "  %-40s %6s   (exempt, no tests)\n" "$pkg" "-"
            go_exempt=$((go_exempt + 1))
        else
            printf "  %-40s %6s   WARN (no test files)\n" "$pkg" "-"
        fi
    fi
done < <(go test -short -cover ./... 2>&1 || true)

cd "$repo_root"

echo ""
echo "Go summary: ${go_passed} passed, ${go_failed} failed, ${go_exempt} exempt"
if [[ "$go_failed" -gt 0 ]]; then
    FAILURES=1
fi
echo ""

# --- Web coverage ---
echo "=== Web Coverage (threshold: 80% — enforced by Vitest) ==="

cd web
if pnpm test:coverage 2>&1; then
    echo "Web coverage: ok"
else
    echo "Web coverage: FAIL"
    FAILURES=1
fi

cd "$repo_root"
echo ""

# --- Summary ---
if [[ "$FAILURES" -gt 0 ]]; then
    if [[ "$STRICT" == "1" ]]; then
        echo "COVERAGE FAILED — strict mode, exiting with error."
        exit 1
    else
        echo "Coverage issues found (advisory mode — not blocking)."
        exit 0
    fi
else
    echo "All coverage checks passed."
    exit 0
fi

# Exempt packages: wiring-only, generated code, test utilities, CLI commands
# Patterns ending with / are prefix matches; others are exact matches.
EXEMPT_PATTERNS=(
    "cmd/"
    "internal/router"
    "internal/server"
    "internal/testutil"
    "internal/version"
    "graph/generated/"
    "graph/model/"
    "migrations"
)

is_exempt() {
    local pkg="$1"
    for pattern in "${EXEMPT_PATTERNS[@]}"; do
        if [[ "$pattern" == */ ]]; then
            # Prefix match
            if [[ "$pkg" == $pattern* || "$pkg" == "${pattern%/}" ]]; then
                return 0
            fi
        else
            # Exact match
            if [[ "$pkg" == "$pattern" ]]; then
                return 0
            fi
        fi
    done
    return 1
}

# Coverage Policy

## Thresholds

| Component | Threshold | Enforcement |
|---|---|---|
| Go packages | 80% line coverage per package | `scripts/ci/check-coverage.sh` |
| Web (React/TS) | 80% lines, functions, branches, statements | Vitest thresholds in `vitest.config.ts` |
| Android | Deferred | Test base not yet credible |
| Browser extension | Deferred | Test base not yet credible |

## Running coverage checks

```bash
make coverage                        # advisory (exit 0 even on failures)
FLOTSAM_STRICT=1 make coverage       # strict (exit 1 on failures)
```

Coverage is also included in the full CI pipeline (`make ci-full` /
`scripts/ci/run-full.sh`).

## Go exempt packages

The following Go packages are exempt from the 80% threshold because they
contain only wiring, generated code, or test utilities:

| Package | Reason |
|---|---|
| `cmd/*` | CLI/server entrypoints — wiring only |
| `internal/router` | HTTP router wiring — covered by integration tests |
| `internal/server` | `http.Server` wrapper — minimal logic |
| `internal/testutil` | Test helpers — not production code |
| `graph/generated` | gqlgen output — never edited manually |
| `graph/model` | gqlgen generated types — never edited manually |

## Web coverage

Web coverage thresholds are defined in `web/vitest.config.ts` and enforced
natively by Vitest. The coverage check script runs `pnpm test:coverage` which
fails if any threshold is not met.

Excluded from web coverage:
- `src/test/**` — test utilities
- `src/**/*.test.{ts,tsx}` — test files themselves
- `src/main.tsx` — app entrypoint
- `src/vite-env.d.ts` — type declarations

## Advisory vs strict mode

By default, coverage checks run in **advisory mode** — failures are reported
but do not block CI. Set `FLOTSAM_STRICT=1` to make coverage failures blocking.

This matches the project-wide CI philosophy: audit-first, with a path to strict
enforcement when coverage is consistently met.

## Deferred: Android and extension

Android and browser extension coverage thresholds are explicitly deferred.
These components do not yet have credible test bases. Thresholds will be added
when their test suites reach a meaningful size (target: 20+ tests with
real assertions).
